mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding aggregate income and consumption expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when PAYEMS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, the discount rate on future corporate cash flows rises sufficiently to compress equity valuations and generate negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and decline in unemployment signal robust demand without wage-pressure constraints, amplifying corporate margin expectations and producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and UNRATE adds no incremental explanatory power (t-statistic below 2.0) versus individual main effects over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, negative month-over-month industrial production growth predicts inventory correction and earnings downgrades, generating negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes in late-cycle periods coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts signal construction sector momentum and downstream demand for durables, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month HOUST changes are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more than 50 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation rising above 3 percent annualized triggers expectations of monetary tightening ahead, increasing real discount rates and producing negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when the CPIAUCSL trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with a low federal funds rate indicates a goldilocks labor market without restrictive monetary policy, generating positive 60-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of falling UNRATE and low FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 60-day returns (win rate below 55 percent).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth accelerating above its trailing trend signals broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate revenues and drives positive monthly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 q/q growth exceeds trailing 2-quarter average by > 0.5pp
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises fail to coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50 percent of cases.
